title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an international public health emergency
summary: The WHO said an Ebola outbreak first seen in the Democratic Republic of the Congo was an international public health emergency, underscoring the world's concerns.

title: Trump blasts ‘disloyal’ Sen. Cassidy while pushing challenger in Louisiana Republican primary
summary: Bill Cassidy, leader of the Senate committee that oversees health policy, is fighting for his political life in Louisiana's Republican primary on Saturday.

title: Opinion: Marty Makary misunderstood something fundamental about the FDA
summary: “The norm-shattering masks a more fundamental misunderstanding of how administrative power can bring about lasting change,” writes Joshua Sharfstein.

title: Opinion: The cloudy truth about ‘blue zones’
summary: “There's been interest in longevity, promoting it, for millennia. But this 25-year campaign really brought it to the forefront,” Eric Topol says of blue zones.

title: STAT+: FDA drug center head Tracy Beth Høeg leaves as agency faces leadership vacuum
summary: The acting director of the FDA’s drug center, Tracy Beth Høeg, is leaving the agency. The news come days after Commissioner Marty Makary handed in his resignation.

title: STAT+: Takeda will pay $13.6 million to settle allegations it paid kickbacks to doctors
summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals agreed to pay $13.6 million to settle allegations that it paid kickbacks to doctors to prescribe an antidepressant.

title: FDA hold puts Aardvark Prader-Willi drug in limbo
summary: The company plans to unblind the late-stage study that was halted due to safety concerns, which could lead to an overhaul of its entire development program.

title: Supreme Court preserves access to abortion pill by mail
summary: The ruling maintains access to the drug while litigation continues. Mifepristone can still be prescribed at pharmacies or by mail without requiring in-person visits.

title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: Biotech leaders campaign for Pazdur; Takeda to lay off 4,500 workers
summary: Hundreds of executives and investors are pushing for Rick Pazdur to be the next FDA commissioner. Elsewhere, Takeda detailed its latest restructuring and Alumis is discarding a once-highly touted…

title: Opinion: First hantavirus, now Ebola: What two outbreaks reveal about global preparedness
summary: “Preparedness is not built during a crisis press conference or after a pathogen has already crossed borders,” writes Krutika Kuppalli.

title: A new Ebola outbreak is confirmed in a remote Congo province, with 65 deaths recorded
summary: A new Ebola outbreak has been confirmed in Congo’s remote Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths recorded so far.

summary: In today's Pharmalittle roundup: U.S. pressuring Germany on drug prices, FDA upheaval and biotech clinical trials, and much more.

title: Opinion: Telehealth abortion with mifepristone continues, with backup plans still in place
summary: Restricting telehealth prescribing of mifepristone wouldn't spell the end of telehealth medication abortion, writes OB-GYN Christine Henneberg.

title: India’s pharma industry is going global. But can it catch up to China?
summary: A record Sun Pharma acquisition signals global ambitions. But infrastructure, capital and policy gaps could stand in the way of India becoming an R&D powerhouse.

title: DOJ accuses Yale and UCLA medical schools of discriminating against white and Asian applicants
summary: The Justice Department sent a letter to Yale School of Medicine on Thursday, alleging it was illegally discriminating against applicants who are not Black or Hispanic.

title: STAT+: Top U.S. officials pressured Germany to pay more for prescription drugs
summary: It's a key piece of Trump's effort to secure lower drug prices for U.S. patients.

summary: A race, two rivals, a photo finish? Former National Human Genome Research Institute archivist explains what Craig Venter tributes are missing.

summary: Why isn't alcohol seen as a public health emergency? Alex Hogan talks to STAT's Isabella Cueto and Lev Facher about their new series on this week's STATus Report.

title: Opinion: I’m 73. I wish my pills wouldn’t keep changing colors and shapes
summary: Pharmaceutical companies, generic manufacturers, and pharmacists should be more careful about changing how pills look.

title: PSA screening for prostate cancer reduces disease-specific deaths, new review shows
summary: Prostate-specific antigen (PSA) blood testing likely reduces the risk of death from prostate cancer, found a new review

title: Supreme Court preserves access to widely used abortion pill, while lawsuit plays out
summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday preserved women’s access to the abortion drug mifepristone, rejecting lower-court restrictions while a lawsuit continues.

title: Create Medicines raises $122M to bolster in vivo CAR-T therapies
summary: The fresh funds will support the biotech's pipeline of RNA-based CAR-T cell therapies for cancer and autoimmune conditions.

title: Biogen pushing tau drug forward despite Alzheimer’s study failure
summary: One of the company’s more closely watched research assets, BIIB080 is now heading to late-stage testing based on “the strength of the biomarker and efficacy data” seen in a Phase 2 trial.

title: With new data, Regenxbio to seek FDA approval of Duchenne gene therapy
summary: Though two serious side effects muddied the results, CEO Curran Simpson expressed optimism about a clearance and claimed FDA leadership will have a “mandate on rare disease flexibility.”

title: FDA’s leadership void leaves biotech with renewed ‘uncertainty’
summary: Marty Makary's resignation leaves the agency with temporary directors in three top positions, and a number of newly instituted policies with an unclear future.

title: Lilly data point to ‘maintenance’ strategies for GLP-1 weight loss
summary: A pair of trial results published in medical journals suggest switching from high-dose injectables to lower doses or pills could help people with obesity keep their weight down over the long term.

title: Assertio accepts new buyout bid; AI biotech Isomorphic banks $2B
summary: Assertio’s latest offer represents a roughly 31% premium over an initial acquisition proposal. Elsewhere, Kyverna is readying a first-of-its-kind approval application and AC Immune is swapping CEOs.
